title: andropogon nardus subsp. hamatulus
intro: Andropogon nardus subsp. hamatulus, also known as Australian citrus or blue grass, is a subspecies of the Andropogon nardus plant, native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Andropogon nardus subsp. hamatulus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs and colds, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties, and it can also be used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments.
